title: Filler BLINDNESS: How To Avoid Your Worst Nightmare
filename: Filler BLINDNESS： How To Avoid Your Worst Nightmare.en.vtt

segments.text: [Music] so feeling for arteries is a really as I've already said a great way of actually turning your anatomical knowledge into reality superficial temporal artery then has anastomosis in most people and this is where the low viscosity problem the two cases of blindness that I'm aware of from Temple injections were both very low viscosity products which makes sense because I've got to go through these anastomosis I think if you're using voluma or something thicker that's more likely it's just going to stop get stuck and you'll have a temple occlusion which you know we can all deal with that with a few of hours of high lays so the very low viscosity products flow through these vessels much more easily and then can flow into the eye you've got the supraorbital artery which also mate will have an estimated but is a smaller vessel normally and then branching off

from underneath the exact boomer you have an exhilarating sorry of transverse facial artery and the infraorbital artery that you can often feel withal not for here so medial to the mid pillory line throwing vessels downwards now we have the submental artery that flows usually just underneath it's like in nature the arteries tend to be hidden where possible if it's a vulnerable spot you'll find the artery normally goes to a place where if you were to be injured is less likely to be cut
